title: Hub on Campus Champaign - Daniel
text:
I have been living at HUB Champaign, and the experience has been consistently uncomfortable due to intrusive and unprofessional conduct from staff, especially around lease renewal pressure.

Starting from the first week after move-in, I already had maintenance issues, my door lock battery died, and a staff member entered my unit for the second time. During that visit, he walked directly down the hallway to my bedroom door and knocked without asking for permission first, which felt extremely inappropriate, especially as an unfamiliar male staff member.

From there, the renewal pressure escalated:
Week 1: multiple renewal marketing emails before I had even settled in.
Week 2: flyers were pushed under every resident’s door without consent.
Week 13: staff began knocking on every unit asking why we were not renewing, pressing residents to give a reason. The interaction felt intrusive and lacked basic courtesy.

On top of that, the building has had repeated issues (door lock failures, leaks, smoke alarm problems), and the tone from certain staff members has often felt dismissive rather than professional.

I DO NOT RECOMMEND anyone to live here. The price does not match the quality of service, maintenance, or the overall living experience.
name: T
responseFromOwnerText:
We're truly sorry to hear that your experience at HUB Champaign has been less than satisfactory. Any instance of unprofessional conduct from our staff is highly unacceptable and we assure you that we take your concerns seriously. We apologize for the discomfort caused by the door lock issues, unexpected visits, and lease renewal pressure you've experienced. The maintenance problems you mentioned are certainly not indicative of the service standard we strive for. Your feedback about intrusive marketing for lease renewals and dismissive attitudes from certain staff members has been duly noted as it helps us improve our amenities and services.

Please accept our deepest apologies if we made you feel uncomfortable or pressured in any way throughout your stay with us. The quality of life for every resident matters greatly to us and it saddens us that yours was negatively impacted during this time period. Please feel free to reach out to our email at Live@HubChampaign.com or call us at 217-318-5770 if you have any further concerns.

title: Hub on Campus Champaign - Daniel
text:
I am a cosignor to a student that lives here for 2 years. Initially chose Hub because of the proximity to campus, but dealing with management is a pain. They will charge you $500+ in move out fees with no proof. I tried asking management for pictures of these damages but they take WEEKS to respond and still fail to provide pictures to this day. The front desk staff are rude to students and unhelpful to parents. If you try calling, the student staff does not know anything and won’t solve your problems.

The rooms were initially nice but this second time round, it was so dirty. We found used condoms and fake nails under the bed. Can’t believe they charged us $120 cleaning fee only to move into a dirty apartment. The lobby is always filled with trash and never cleaned. No security whatsoever at night, believe what people say about the building being unsafe. Packages have gone missing more than twice in a year.

Washer and dryer sucks. They never wash & dry clothes properly. Maintenance also takes weeks before they come around to fix it. There is trash all over the halls on the carpets and foul smell if you live by the chute.

For the price we pay, I expect better service and cleaner apartments. The only good thing about Hub is its location. Will not be coming back and I suggest you don’t either. Definitely a bait & switch case here and all the good reviews are only so students can get gift cards & snacks. Save your money!

text: Bait and Switch Alert!!!  Be careful!!  We had a  bad experience with these guys.  We submitted the application and paid the application fee and were then told that the floorplans were no longer available and pushed us to the more expensive options.  They refused to refund the application fee and would not connect me with a manager when I asked.  Bait and Switch!!!  Bad business practices!!!
name: Andrew K
responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your experience didn’t meet your expectations, and we’d like to clarify a few points for transparency.

All of our floorplans are subject to availability, which is communicated during the application process. Submitting an application and paying the application fee initiates our screening process, but does not guarantee a specific unit. In your case, the floorplan you initially applied for was no longer available by the time your application was processed—a possibility we disclose upfront.

We did present you with multiple alternative options, including some at a lower price point than the unit you originally applied for. However, you indicated you were not interested in those. While we understand your disappointment, the application fee covers the cost of credit and background screening and, as clearly stated in our policies, is non-refundable once processing has begun.

We always aim to be fair and transparent, and we’re available to answer questions for future applicants to ensure they feel confident before applying.

If you don't order stuff a lot, you will be fine.

title: Hub on Campus Champaign - Daniel
text:
TL;DR: thin walls, no soundproofing, bad roommate matching, maintenance was subpar. Only benefit was I never heard the people above me walking. They do giveaways where you enter by posting a Google review- in my opinion that is making their rating artificially high.

The walls are thin, we could hear our neighbor’s alarm going off for HOURS every morning. Some units constantly have parties, management doesn’t care about noise complaints lol.

If you happen to do random roommate matching, they will not care about the survey you fill out. They paired me and my friend (who is asthmatic and reacts heavily to weed) with two smokers. I honestly believe they just paired us up because we were all the same type of Asian.

When we were finally forced to go to management about one roommate who was a heavy smoker (literally had the entire apartment reeking of weed) , they did nothing even though there was a “no smoking” clause in our lease. That same roommate added a cat into the lease without asking us, was dirty and didn’t clean up after the cat- again management did nothing despite there being a clause in the lease about maintaining clean common areas. They also wouldn’t let our roommate with cat allergies switch units even after explaining the situation. But allegedly they let a different girl switch units- and the only difference was that the other girl was a sorority girl.

At move out all roommates were charged for the cat damage because there was apparently “no cat on the lease”- despite having been told BY THE FRONT DESK that the cat was in the lease when we first inquired about switching units. Despite showing evidence that the damage was caused by a cat, they charged us all over $700 because the cat owner didn’t “own up to it.”

At one point, our hallway regularly flooded and maintenance tried to blame it on us leaving the sink on. Only when we called the city to check it out did they magically find a way to stop the flooding.

There was a point where people would get stuck in the elevator for a few minutes until someone else called the elevator to another floor. I have no idea if/when the remedied that.

Events rarely happen, when they do they’re small only a small amount of people can join and they run out of items.

They charged me and my roommates different rent prices for the same apartment. Not based on room square footage or anything, simply just based on vibes. The trash room is atrocious- at times the door to the chute locks and the trash ends up piling up in the room.

They don’t have mesh in the windows, so if you open the window for fresh air you will 100% get bugs inside.

Not sure why their rating is so high- but I feel like it has to do with the fact they do giveaways where leaving a review is an entry.
name: Rockelle F
responseFromOwnerText: We sincerely regret the displeasure you've experienced and would like to assure you that your concerns have been noted. Our team is dedicated to improving our service and providing a more comfortable living experience for our residents moving forward.

title: Hub on Campus Champaign - Daniel
text: After living at the Hub, I can confidently say it’s one of the best apartment complexes in the area. From the amenities, to the staff, to the community, it is the perfect place for a college student to live. The pool is awesome when it's nice out, the gym has a great variety of equipment, and the study spaces are a great for when I want a change of scenery to study without having to leave the building. The staff is incredibly friendly and responsive. Our maintenance requests are addressed promptly, and the staff always goes above and beyond to ensure residents are happy. Joey from the front desk is always so cheerful on the job and eager to help anyone with anything they need. You can also tell that the leasing manager Kaitlyn creates a positive work environment for the rest of the staff. She is always willing to drop anything she is doing to help the residents, and puts together awesome giveaways and events. The location also couldn’t be more convenient with it being close to the bookstore and the quad. Overall, I have and will continue to highly recommend The Hub to anyone looking for an on campus apartment!

title: Hub on Campus Champaign - Daniel
text:
The Hub Champaign is hands down one of the best housing experiences I’ve ever had! From the moment I toured the building, I could tell this wasn’t your average student apartment—it’s a home away from home with all the perks you could possibly want.

First, the location is unbeatable. It’s right in the heart of Champaign, so getting to campus, dining spots, or nightlife is incredibly convenient. But what really sets The Hub apart are the amenities and the attention to detail. The rooftop pool and hot tub are absolutely stunning, offering a perfect place to unwind or hang out with friends after a long week. Plus, the views from up there? Breathtaking!

The apartments themselves are modern and thoughtfully designed. I’ve been so impressed with how spacious and well-furnished the units are, with high-quality finishes that make it feel more like a luxury condo than student housing. The in-unit washer and dryer are a game-changer, and the smart technology features, like keyless entry, add a nice touch of convenience.

Another highlight is the building’s sense of community. The management team deserves a special shoutout—they are so friendly, responsive, and genuinely care about the residents. They regularly organize fun events, like game nights and food giveaways, that make it easy to meet neighbors and feel connected. Maintenance requests are handled promptly, which has been such a relief compared to other places I’ve lived.

For someone who needs a good study spot, the co-working spaces and private study rooms are perfect. They’re quiet, comfortable, and well-equipped, which has made staying productive so much easier. And for those late-night cravings? The coffee bar has you covered!

Lastly, the gym is impressive—it’s not just an afterthought like at other apartments. It’s fully stocked with everything from free weights to Peloton bikes, and it’s open 24/7, so it works with my busy schedule.

Living at The Hub Champaign has truly enhanced my college experience. It’s the perfect blend of comfort, luxury, and convenience. If you’re considering living here, don’t hesitate—it’s worth every penny!

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I have quite enjoyed my experience living here. The apartment is nice and pretty well sound proofed which is a big thing for me. The maintenance and leasing office staff are nice and get to most problems very quickly. The biggest gripe and why I have lowered the stars even though I love living here is with the parking garage and security concerns.

One of the biggest things is that it is basically impossible to reach the leasing office unless you go in person. The people are helpful if you're able to get to them but any time I have tried calling when the office is open no one picks up. Sometimes the office will close early or not be open at all with no warning. Any time I have tried calling both the phone numbers provided the phone will ring and you'll be connected to their AI assistant Sam which how does that help anyone?? I've never been locked out but it worries me that if I could not get ahold of them for a simple problem then what would happen if I did?

The second is that I've brought up to the office time and time again about the parking garage as I pay a lot of money to park my car in there both for safety of the car and mainly myself. When I toured I was promised the garage would have secured parking gate where you would need a scanner to enter and that sounded great. Not only was the gate not ready before then but it took 3 months with asking the leasing office about it to actually get the gate working. I asked about getting compensation during this time as I was paying for something I wasn't receiving but was rejected. This is also to mention that the gate breaks often and would be left open for anyone to enter or park their car in and by the time of writing this review the gate is STILL BROKEN with no updates from management. This might not sound like a big deal but there have been people smoking in the garage, there have been homeless people camping in there, and I have had things stolen out of the garage to little concern of the management when I reported it to them. Security is a big concern of mine and I wish it was taken more seriously here. Another problem while not too related to security is that there is a million delivery drivers who will park their cars in the garage, in front of the garage, or in the entrance to the parking lot behind the building and they block the way in or out so you cannot leave. While I understand that's not something the management can really fix I'm surprised it's a problem that I deal with weekly and it is very annoying. I'm hoping these are problems that can be fixed in the last few months of me living here but I'm not too hopeful. I feel that the amount I pay to live here these should be non-issues.

The lease transfer / subleasing policy is outrageous. If you need to move out early, you are basically forced to lose the equivalent of THREE MONTHS of rent. This is completely unreasonable and absolutely not renter-friendly. What makes it worse is that this was not clearly explained or emphasized at signing — you only find out how bad it is once you’re already stuck.

On top of that, the HVAC system is a disaster. The air conditioning / heating breaks repeatedly during the winter, when you actually need heat to live comfortably. I’ve dealt with constant issues, unreliable fixes, and no permanent solution. Living here during colder months was genuinely miserable.

For the price they charge, this place delivers terrible management, terrible maintenance, and zero accountability. They are happy to take your money, but when problems come up, you are on your own.

Save yourself the stress, money, and frustration. There are far better apartments out there. I regret choosing this place and would never live here again.

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I’ve never been more disappointed and infuriated with an apartment management team in my life. Latitude Apartment has shown nothing but greed, hypocrisy, and complete disrespect for its tenants.

First of all, I renewed my lease for 2026–2027, but before that lease even took effect, I asked to cancel it. Guess what? They refused to let me terminate it. When I tried to find a sublease instead, they had the audacity to tell me I’d need to pay three full months of rent just to do so. Three months! Every other apartment I’ve ever heard of charges a reasonable flat sublease fee — usually around $500, not thousands of dollars. Latitude’s policy is nothing short of predatory and exploitative.

As if that wasn’t insulting enough, they recently started advertising a $1250 renewal bonus for the same 2026–2027 term. Naturally, I asked if I could receive it too, since I had renewed earlier — in good faith. Their response? A flat-out no. They said my “benefit” was that my rent wouldn’t increase. But when I asked for the current rent rate, I found out it’s actually lower than what I’m paying now. So not only did I get no bonus, I’m paying more than new tenants. It’s absolutely absurd and completely unfair.

And the way their staff handled everything made it even worse. The attitude was rude, dismissive, and completely unprofessional. One staff member even told me not to come to the office to talk about it and to “just send an email.” But anyone who’s dealt with Latitude knows that emails are almost never answered — you can send ten and be lucky to get one response back.

Latitude Apartment clearly doesn’t care about its residents. They care only about squeezing as much money as possible out of people and hiding behind “policy” whenever they’re confronted. I’m utterly disgusted and deeply regret ever signing a lease with them. If you value fairness, honesty, and basic human decency, stay far away from this place.

Our policies are crafted to promote consistency and fairness, though we acknowledge that individual situations can vary. We are dedicated to enhancing our communication and service moving forward. If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out. We are here and ready to assist you! --Latitude Apartments Team

title: Latitude Apartments
text: Honestly, this place is a scam. Management will find every possible way to keep your money. The “security deposit” is basically two months of rent you’ll never get back, and if you try to sublease, get ready to lose around three months’ rent in total. Their renewal deals are a joke — the “bonus” changes all the time with no clear rule. If you renew early, you might miss out on over $1,000 compared to people who wait. The building is super old, nothing gets updated, and if your unit faces the campus side, you’ll never sleep because of motorcycle and car noise all night. Somehow they still charge a $20 “premium view” fee for that noisy view. Save yourself the stress — there are way better places nearby with honest management and quieter surroundings.

title: Latitude Apartments
text: PLEASE READ ENTIRE THING. Latitude apartments is the worst apartment I've ever lived at. All they want is to take your money. The front desk staff are extremely rude and you will pay tooth and nail for the worst conditions. I have a feeling they are BUYING good reviews. They have lost over 10+ of my packages and mocked me in the process. Front desk and management will actively gaslight you into thinking they are not responsible when literally their package system is broken. They will blame anyone but themselves. They do not answer phone calls, rarely answer emails, and can only be reached if you talk to them in person. Even then, they will likely give you an attitude. I have anxiety every time I speak to them. I paid $1900/month for an apartment that was promised to me to be cleaned but was actually broken, full of dog hair, and disgusting. I had to personally hire carpet cleaners, maids, and needed to bug maintenance to get out fixed. There were $200+ of HIDDEN fees EVERY MONTH. The amenities are always broken and very dirty. No blame to the maintenance staff however, they seem like nice people. On top of that, my apartment was unhealthy to live in. It smelled terrible ALL YEAR and they gaslighted me into thinking it was fine. By the summer, I discovered mold growing on almost all my stuff due to humidity and bad air in the apartment. When I moved, I realized EVERYTHING I OWNED SMELLED-INCLUDING ME. Like other reviews say, they will try to charge you enormous amounts for almost anything- seemingly fictitious and you will have no say ... Just a petty fight with some "management" younger than you. AND THERE WAS NO SOAP IN THE WOMEN'S PUBLIC RESTROOM FOR ALMOST THE ENTIRE YEAR- even after I told them. Half the time, they do not do what they say they will. I know they will read this so I hope they don't take it out on me financially but to some degree, they should understand. They hurt me financially (A LOT), they hurt my health (a lot of nights I couldn't breathe and later I found out it was mold), they disrespected me repeatedly, and also caused me a lot of trouble- and in the end, still seeing how they can milk more money from you... Like you are an object for use. When I saw new students with parents coming in- I wanted so badly to tell them to run as they were signing with a "friendly" staff but I thought it wasn't my place.... Edit: I realized I am still dealing with the health effects of mold right now. I believe I was subject to mycotoxins all year and I'm struggling to fix my health.

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
Don’t move in here! First, you’ll be charged for all sorts of unreasonable things. I really don’t understand why I’m being told I have to pay a $200 cleaning fee when there was no damage, and I had already spent two days cleaning before I left. You’ll never really know what that $200 was supposedly for, it just magically disappears into their pocket.

Fine. I understand that apartments will squeeze every extra fee they possibly can, so I’m not even going to waste my time arguing about that. But what truly amazes me is that when I went back to the office to simply ask a question about my package, the woman working there decided to put on quite a show. She glared at me like I had personally ruined her day, told me it “wasn’t her problem,” cut me off three times before I could even finish my sentence, and made it very clear she had zero interest in helping. So I gave up and left, because clearly, my $1,500 a month in rent was never enough to buy even basic courtesy. Is this what passes for “customer service” here? It’s almost impressive how quickly she managed to remind me that once you’ve moved out, you’re basically invisible, after all, why bother being polite to someone you can’t bill anymore?😅

At this price, you can definitely find a much better apartment and better service in Champaign, so why come here just to be treated badly?

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I deeply regret signing a lease with Latitude Apartments. After applying, I found myself in a financial predicament and tried to cancel the lease within 72 hours. Contrary to what should have been, they approved my lease within minutes of my cancellation request, which should not have been legally binding. Adding insult to injury, the rent for the identical apartment model was slashed by $176/month just a dozen days after my lease agreement, with no prior notice or offer to adjust my rate. To date, they have dismissed all my reasonable requests.

This entire ordeal has been a massive letdown, and I urge extreme caution to potential tenants. Their lack of transparency and failure to deal fairly has left me with significant regrets about my decision.
name: Jiayi Huang
responseFromOwnerText: We are genuinely sorry to learn about your experience and empathize with your concerns. Our commitment is to uphold transparent and honest communication with all our residents. We acknowledge any confusion surrounding the lease cancellation and rent adjustment. Your feedback is highly valued, and we will leverage it to enhance our procedures. Should you require any clarification, please feel free to contact us. Our team is here and eager to support you.

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I just moved in and the place is pretty nice. Honestly, it exceeded my expectations. I thought my 1-bedroom was going to be too small, but it's actually nice. The Wi-Fi is stupid fast, which is important for me because I work remotely. Additionally, I also appreciate the good air conditioning because my work computer can get hot. So far, the management team has been professional. In particular, Layla, Ciara, and Jasmine were chill. Good job, Latitude.

Edit (3 Stars): I have to change my review to 3-stars. AC isn't working, which not only makes me feel uncomfortable, but my work computer relies on cool air. It's currently 89 degrees in my unit... And the Wifi is suddenly acting peculiar now. I pay over $1600/mo to avoid these issues and so I should expect this stuff to be dealt with as soon as possible.

Despite my disappointment with the amenities, I do want to mention a maintenance gentlemen who works there is really cool.

Edit (1 Star): Definitely don't recommend this place, they randomly raised my rent to nearly $2000/mo when it should've be $1650/mo. I've had over $500 worth of amazon packages stolen, their cameras in the package room apparently doesn't work and the security guard (that I'm paying for) is nonexistent. The hallway smells terrible. The floors are disgusting. I genuinely believe they robbed me
name: Kinetic Motive
responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you so much for your kind review! We love our team and are so happy to hear of your positive  experience! We look forward to continuing to make Latitude your home! 
Your Team at Latitude

textTranslated:
Hello everyone!!!
I've been living in this residential complex since May!!!
Everything is super and great here.
The staff and workers are very friendly.
I came from Kazakhstan.

title: 75 Armory
text:
My daughter lived in a 3 bedroom for 2 years. As other have stated- furniture/appliances look new, deliveries ( especially amazon) can be a nightmare: missing packages, not a secure space- packages just thrown around. Maintenance unresponsive, noise level depends on where you're located. The single trash bin outside is constantly overflowing- they need to get another one. The second floor room "the nest" is downright scary when it rains or slight amount of wind- poor construction material choices for this space. (they drew straws to determine who ended up with this room)

The real kicker is when you leave: management not only takes your security deposit but charges each tenant a lot of money- over $300 a person-  to repaint the entire apartment, steam clean carpets, etc. I was there when they moved out and the place was clean, the walls and floors were not scuffed or dirty, no stains etc.

Management wants the place to look new and fresh for the next tenants but the previous tenants should not have to pay for a total new look. This should be a management cost!

I've had several kids in different apartments over the years and this is the first time I had to pay a large excessive amount of money after moving out. Look for other options!
name: Mary Jo Le

title: 75 Armory
text:
Package stealing, service attitude with little empathy and accountability, noise…
But the room is relatively large, clean and new. The furniture is fine. Maintenance is fine. Convenient transportation and close to the college I study.
name: Zephyr H

title: 75 Armory
text: Move in condition was filthy. Everything had dust and grime on it. Ceilings in bath/shower have patches looking like will cave in at any minute.  Dents on most of appliances, food still in freezer from last tenant. Door knobs holes in Walls and doors.  Was not painted recently. Front door has gap like it was pryed open before. Not given any kind of opener for garage and no answer as to when we will only told Garage doors do not close for next few weeks. Safety in this building in horrible . No way this place was cleaned or even checked by maintenance or leasing. Don’t rent with them.
name: john

title: 75 Armory
text: Noise level is surprisingly very calm even despite it's location near where the marching band practices and the adjacent frats/sororities. Good furnishings for the price and spacious living room + kitchen space. Only thing that keeps me from giving it a 5 is due to maintenance being slower than other property management companies I've been with, but not unreasonably so. Additionally, my unit wasn't that clean when I moved in, though I'm not sure if that was just a one-off.
name: Delbert Su

title: 75 Armory
text: Highly recommend staying here if you look forward to doing your clothes at a laundromat with the homeless. Unresponsive management who doesn't fix anything.
name: Nick Carlson

title: 75 Armory
text:
Edit: 0/5.
Had a BIRD come in through the dryer vent while we were on away during Christmas, pooped all over our personal items, had to clean it ourselves and replace everything while management said "oh we'll credit you for it" which turned into "let me talk to my regional manager first" which turned into silence. Literally can't make this up.

Very spotty wifi, slow maintenance, takes them 3 years to fix the garage door, doors left broken, showerheads cracked. A lot of the people that actually live here are nice and take care of their property, but they have their own horror stories. Maintenance requests often get marked as completed even when no one steps foot in the apartment, but when they DO show up the Maintenance guys are super nice, quick, and know what they're doing.

Management is always hard to reach, the office is downtown, which is smart bc if it was closer I'd be in there every weekend to call them out. Cancelled a friend's lease w/o their specific consent. Tight parking and roommate scratched car pretty bad on the yellow guard posts bc they're so close. Went up a bunch in price even though the quality of service took a nosedive, well furnished, really love the floor plan (very spacious), and it's at a great location, it's honestly night and day from 2 years ago, and i guess they stopped caring, they'd sell the property to a competent company. Or honestly hire me please, students deserve better. (also most of the new positive reviews are literally employees and they don't even make the effort to change the names).
name: Jacob Adrian

title: 75 Armory
text: Please do not live here. Ever since the new management came this place is an utter disappointment. My in unit dryer was broken and not replaced for over 2 MONTHS. The management never gave us any answers or compensation for their lack of responsibility. My toilet flusher was held together with tape and sticks which I had to go out and replace myself. The AC constantly broke as did many of the lights in the kitchen and living room. Their were boogers smeared on the wall of the bathroom when I first moved in it was absolutely disgusting. They emailed our residents 15 times about how they were working on our garage door to be fixed and a year later it’s still inoperable after multiple people had their cars robbed and parts stolen off of them. The electricity bill gets increased to 3x the amount it should be during breaks so they can make money. Absolutely DO NOT live here you’ll regret it
name: Madison Humanicki

title: 75 Armory
text: Customer service was a 10/10! Bri was so light hearted and helpful having an answer for all my questions! Her positive attitude made the experience 10x better!
name: Virtuous Thomas

title: 75 Armory
text: Briana in the leasing office was so great and helpful, the apartment was so nice and she really took the time to show me around, the amenities and what places were around.
name: Jennifer Johnson

title: 75 Armory
text:
Briana was very helpful with all of the information I needed on getting my very first apartment. She was detailed and very informative without me having to ask. Thanks a lot Briana :)

CJ
name: Cameron Johnson

title: 75 Armory
text:
DON'T BE FOOLED!!! Though this apartment has nice spaces/furnishings the management is genuinely so abismal you should avoid living here at all costs.

My washer and dryer have been broken since August 29th and it's November 1st. Things in the apartment are cheap and always falling apart. Management closes their office at random times without notifying tenants and often does not pick up calls or sources them to people in different states who know nothing about the property. They can't provide any updates on when maintenance issues will be fixed and currently have not responded to my last 4 emails. I'm pretty convinced they've just given up completely on fixing all the maintenance problems.

There are also huge security problems at this property like the front and back doors never being locked and the garage to the private parking never being closed.

The property is also very dirty. There is trash juice in the elevator on a daily basis and piles of trash accumulate on every floor.

Oh and my car got towed from my own spot that I pay for due to 75 E Armory's negligence. I had to have management on speaker phone while I stood with the towing company so they could flusteredly own up to their blunder and get me my car back. (reference photo 1 <3)

There are sooooo many other places to live on campus with better management than this. (Go for JSM management!) Avoid 75 E. Armory and Next Chapter Properties at all costs. Beware!!
name: Erin Lee

title: 75 Armory
text: Our washer has been broken for two months and our maintenance requests were deleted and not fulfilled. The garage doors are always open, and the company has not done anything about it despite multiple complaints to close it. The entrance doors are always unlocked. This causes many packages and items in cars to be stolen. It’s also a safety risk for all the residents living here. They are very bad at answering calls, and they never answer emails. They try to charge you for unnecessary things and when you call them out on it, they delete the charges. This shows how unethical the charges were to begin with, and it shows how terrible the management is. Communication with the company is very bad and maintenance requests take forever to complete. Do not rent an apartment here, it’s not worth your money.
name: A N

title: 75 Armory
text: my washer and dryer has been stolen for over a month and they have done absolutely nothing to fix it and have no intention to. this place is the worst!
name: Maggie Hughes

title: 75 Armory
text: This complex and company is absolutely horrible. DO NOT LIVE HERE!!! Our washer and dryer has been broken for months and they refuse to fix it. All of our appliances are falling apart and no one in the leasing office or on the maintenance team answers our calls. The unit was filthy when we moved in, their were previous resident’s socks stuffed between my closet doors and gym smeared into my carpet. The garage doors have never been down a single time and my car has already been broken into. They refused to release the security footage and told that they were sorry it happened but there’s nothing they could do. The front doors are broken and don’t lock. There is no security or help at all, they’ve told us multiple times to fix our problems ourselves without any financial compensation. Most of the residents here right now are pursuing legal action.
name: Mary L

title: 75 Armory
text:
This is my third year here and it is only because I needed to not be without a home for weeks due to the stupid campus housing logistics. The staff has gotten worse each year. This place is only good if you're willing to sacrifice security among other things to pay for things we don't even benefit from. It is ridiculous that they charge us the same rent as the properties that have a gym that we cannot have access to.

The building is not secure at all. The garage has been not working for at least a year. making the building not secure at all which is part of the reason I signed with them. They have issues with squirrels eating wires but this is due to the building not being up to code, and even if there is an exception here, it is a very simple solution to their problem but they aren't willing to put in any money to help us. Due to the lack of security, we had our catalytic converter stolen from the car and they did not seem to care and could not provide us with any help with the police and the fact that they had nothing to say even though it is their fault, we paid a lot to fix the damage done to the car.

We have been lucky enough to not have any major issues, but I know that they do not have a good track record of fixing things. The back door has been broken for over a year and they act like we don't know how to use it instead of acknowledging that something is broken. I don't know if a member of the office staff has set foot in the property since they started their jobs.

I wish I did not resign here, but it was all that I could afford to have parking and laundry. The staff needs a wake-up call here. Please do not sign if you are looking for places next year, I can't wait to never rent a place on campus again when I leave
name: Stephanie Bean

title: 75 Armory
text:
My advice is to never rent from this property company or any of their locations. Early on in my lease I woke up to maggots covering my entire living room from a dead bird stuck on the balcony above me. Eventually maintenance workers came and told me to keep killing the maggots myself. Orkin came but that did not resolve the problem. I killed as many as I could but the maggots later turned into a fly problem.

I also payed for a parking space all year. The spaces are typically numbered and sectioned off. My "parking space" was in between two real parking spaces and a pole. My "space" was crossed in yellow lines clearly indicating that people were not supposed to park there.

At one point my entire window fell out on top of me when I was sleeping. I know my roommate had a leak in his ceiling that was never taken care of. The walls were so thin that I could hear my neighbors' conversations even when they were talking at a normal volume. Additionally, management was no help at all and was next to impossible to speak with. Finally, most of my maintenance issues were never fixed but the tickets were deleted as though they were. I could go on but this paints a sufficient picture.

I do not normally complain and I have never written a review on Google. However, the conditions at 75 Armory are so laughably bad that it makes you wonder if management cares about their tenants at all or are truly incompetent at their jobs. I'm unsure of which is worse.
name: Tyler Goldberg

title: 75 Armory
text: I've lived here for 2 years now, and although the location is great, they do not answer maintenance requests in a timely manner. I've never had a problem with them until this year, but I've put in a work order literally over 3 months ago and they still haven't come to fix it. I've called the office 3 times, and each time they nicely say that they'll let the maintenance guy know, but never apologize for the ridiculous amount of time it's been. The last time I called the assistant manager said, "there's no guarantee" that maintenance can get to it by this week after I've waited for 3 months. There's no sense of urgency. Not to mention my washer being broken for months and them not having a solution to that besides me having to take my laundry elsewhere when I am paying for this amenity - this happened to another woman living here too. They do not do a good job of keeping their residents happy here. I can go on. Their management has really gone downhill since a couple of people left this company in the fall. For the price I'm paying to live here, residents deserve better treatment.
name: Diane Barrios-Smith

title: 75 Armory
text: Something is missing here…
name: Annamarie Olsen

title: 75 Armory
text:
But really 0 stars! I've have problems with this management since the day I signed the lease. From trying to force me to live with tenants with Covid-19, to attempting to remove me from my current apartment and now I find my 1 month "gift credit" was misapplied. At every turn, there is a problem by fault of management to which they can offer no adequate remedy. When almost forced to move in with tenants with the COVID virus, management couldn't offer any assistance but for me to cancel the lease and sign another for a higher rent (of course). When I was given the free month, I was told I could use it either for one month or across the lease term, but based on my discretion. Today I find out after months of thinking I was paying a discounted rate, that the credit was misapplied and Im supposed to be paying a higher amount. I should also mention that about 3 months into the lease, I called to confirm whether the credit was in effect, and given confirmation that it was. I've had it with this place and with every complaint, management offers no assistance or assistance in the form of  a band-aid that is ineffective. I can guarantee there are other management groups better suited for whatever your needs are, even a motel might provide you better customer service.

Update: they threw out my entire apartment bc they mistakenly categorized me as a move out instead of renewal. I had to rebuy everything I owned. Def never returning to this place. whatever management they have is a joke.
name: Osayame O

title: 75 Armory
text: Terrible management.
name: Ryan Kang

title: 75 Armory
text: 75 Armory is a great place to live and it is very accommodating. The leasing process is simple to navigate and the staff are available to guide you through it. I would definitely recommend 75 Armory for any students wanting to rent an apartment at UIUC.

title: 75 Armory
text: Love this property, and management was great too!

text:
75 Armory is probably the best place for new students at UIUC. Representatives continuously do their best to answer all questions on the same day, and armory’s portal for residents is simple and efficient.
I moved in this week and the subsequent contact has been impeccable: an outstanding attention to make us feel comfortable and safe in this new place. I totally recommend to all who are looking for a serious, convenient and kind service.

title: 75 Armory
text: Great location and beautiful updates!

title: 75 Armory
text: This location is awesome! It's really close to a bunch of stuff on campus which is great. The staff was also super helpful and responsive when I needed them. I highly recommend it!

text: 75 has been great! Lots of storage, really clean, and furniture/appliances are modern and up to date. title: 75 Armory
text: 75 armory is a decent apartment building. My apartment was completely dirty upon move in (super dusty, cabinets covered in black grease, food caked on drawer handles, drains clogged with hair and stains on couch). Other than that, the appliances work well and the room is sutable for my needs.

name: Sylvain Turcotte

title: 75 Armory
text: I can not stress how poor the management is. Everything in this apartment breaks and they are in no hurry to fix it. My balcony door isn't sealed so there is always a cold draft and water leaks all over the living room floor when it rains. The thermostat is broken leaving our apartment at unbearable cold and hot temperatures. The garage door broke in December- we are now half way through April and the issue hasn't been fixed- one of the garage doors constantly stays open. There have been no updates about this. Anyone who signed here expecting security did not get what they paid for because anyone can get right in through the garage- which is exactly what happened last night. Someone broke in, vandalized the building (including shattering the front door), and had to be arrested. 75 Armory has been in no hurry to fix this problem for nearly 4 months now. The communication between management and the residents is even worse. They moved a new roommate into our apartment and sent us an email 5 minutes prior to her coming in. A little notice would have been nice. Not only that, they gave one of my roommates an expired parking sticker, towed her, made her pay to get the car back, and made her buy a new parking sticker- a roughly $800 expense for something that was completely their fault. Seriously! The fire alarm went off at 3 am and 5 am 3 nights in a row on some of the coldest days of the year causing all of the residents to leave their rooms and go stand in the freezing cold for 20-30 minutes before the fire department got there. This is an issue that should have been resolved after the first night. In the winter the FOB to get into the building stopped working for many residents- but luckily we could get in through the OPEN GARAGE DOOR!!! They are impossible to get ahold of, even when you call or email they don't respond. They don't salt the sidewalk area or entrance area, I slipped on the stairs and had a bruise on my hip that lasted 3 weeks. I would not recommend ANYONE to live in this building.
name: Hailey Gaba

title: 75 Armory

title: 75 Armory
text: I've ran into some of the leasing staff on the street and they were always nice and asked how my stay was which really made my experience. The washer and dryer are a little small, but compared to other places I've liked at they aren't that different. Maintenance was always really nice too and answered any of the questions I had.
name: Jake Lewis

title: 75 Armory
text:
The apartment itself is 4 stars, don't get me wrong. Much better layout and design than most places on campus. Furnishing is great, only real downfall is the outdated washer/dryer and TV setup (but Comcast is provided) for the monthly asking price.

What brings it down is more on the management and outside the apartment itself. Maintenance was quick for most issues, but BLACK MOLD was growing above my shower this last semester and was never fixed. maintenance came more than once, but clearly management didn't make this a priority. Multiple requests to fix resulted in simple temporary fixes. I don't work there and can't say how they operate, but it was a 5 month lease so to us it seemed like they were pushing it back until we were moved out to avoid conflict with rent.

Another common issue was the handling of delivered packages. Again, another scenario where I don't know how it operates, but two of my packages delivered to my door were roughly banged up. I noticed throughout the school semester packages at the doors of other tenants were also damaged. I'm not sure if this is the delivery company's doing or related towards 75 Armory, regardless, they should have received enough complaints to fix this issue (didn't seem to change).

Lastly, if you have a parking spot (that is slightly overpriced compared to most buildings) you better make sure your sticker is on there!! A friend of mine had the temporary one and still got towed and had to pay the $350 himself!! Terrible system, letting the towing company inside the garage for free range picking of cars. They tow any car that doesn't have a sticker, even if the owner of the car pays for the spot, as opposed to most systems that leave it up to the parking spot owner's discretion to tow or not. It was in the contract (permanent sticker required) so nothing my friend could do. We were informed at the beginning of the year that 75 would provide permanent stickers in our rooms, I never received mine and had to go into the office a few weeks later to get mine. Clearly 75 has some flaws and fails to show its concern for tenants.
name: Tony Frank

title: 75 Armory
text:
Aside from the location, that’s just about all this apartment has to offer. The leasing office is at another site which makes dealing with management very inconvenient. Management is also very poor as they rarely will help you with an issue. A lot of the ammentites have issues from washers to cracked doors when I moved in. They seem selective on the work orders they fix because I’ve had some issues resolved and others not.

The tv is very poor quality and has a less than basic cable package. Finally, it is pretty overpriced for an apartment in general, let alone a poor one. If I could sign elsewhere, I would definitely consider.

title: 75 Armory
text:
Review of Next Chapter Properties, the landlord for 75 Armory:
Stay away from Next Chapter Properties at all costs. I leased with them at another location last year and while the buildings looked nice, NCP is woefully unfit to be a landlord. They run a well known security deposit scam and have no concept of the legal system. They will illegally steal your security deposit and claim you must pay absurd fees for damages and cleaning caused by normal wear and tear. This is illegal on several counts (Urbana Codes Sec. 12.5-20).

Due to their incompetence at running a business, they even had to hire a lawyer to deal with all of the complaints they receive every year from tenants who have figured out the scheme they are running. In a phone call with them about the issue, they didn't even tried to hide the fact that they defraud and do this to "99% of our tenants." Next Chapter Properties is objectively unfit to operate at such a prestigious university. There are plenty of other landlords who operate ethically and will not defraud you.
name: William Gayde

title: 75 Armory
text: Really nice apartments and great management company
